enrichment.short_description: Savio centralizes customer feature requests from Slack, HubSpot, Intercom, and more so teams can prioritize by revenue.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Savio centralizes feature requests from HubSpot, Intercom, Slack, and more, letting product teams prioritize by ARR and votes.

features.title: Centralized feedback collection
features.description: Aggregates feature requests from Slack, HubSpot, Intercom, Zendesk, Salesforce, and Help Scout into one inbox.

features.title: Revenue-based prioritization
features.description: Sorts requests by number of requesters, total ARR, opportunity value, and account MRR.

features.title: CRM data filtering
features.description: Filters and segments feature requests using customer data pulled from HubSpot or Salesforce.

features.title: Structured intake forms
features.description: Custom fields let Sales and Customer Success teams log feedback consistently.

features.title: Dev tool sync
features.description: Pushes feature-request status updates from Jira, Linear, or Shortcut back into the connected CRM.

features.title: Public voting boards
features.description: Lets customers vote on and follow feature requests directly.

pros: Ties feature requests directly to ARR and account value for revenue-driven prioritization
Wide range of native integrations, including HubSpot, Salesforce, Intercom, Zendesk, Slack, and Help Scout
Unlimited free (non-admin) users on every plan
14-day free trial with no credit card required
cons: Entry-level Essential plan includes only 1 paid user before per-seat charges apply
CRM integrations (HubSpot, Salesforce) are limited to the higher Business tier
Enterprise pricing is not published and requires contacting sales

faqs.question: Does Savio offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, Savio does not offer a permanently free plan, but it provides a 14-day free trial with no credit card required, plus unlimited free (non-admin) users on every paid plan.

faqs.question: What tools does Savio integrate with?
faqs.answer: Savio integrates with Slack, HubSpot, Intercom, Zendesk, Salesforce, Help Scout, Jira, Linear, Shortcut, Segment.com, and Zapier, plus a Chrome extension and API.

faqs.question: How much does Savio cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at $39/month for Essential, with Professional at $79/month and Business at $249/month; Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: Can Sales and Customer Success teams use Savio?
faqs.answer: Yes, Savio includes structured data-entry fields designed for Sales and Success teams to log customer feedback.

use_cases.title: Revenue-driven roadmap prioritization
use_cases.description: Product teams rank feature requests by total ARR and requester count to decide what to build next.

use_cases.title: Consolidating scattered feedback
use_cases.description: Teams pull feature requests out of Slack threads, support tickets, and CRM notes into one searchable system.

use_cases.title: Sales-to-product feedback loop
use_cases.description: Sales reps log feature gaps encountered in deals so product teams can see which requests affect revenue.

features: Zendesk, Zapier, API, Shortcut, Jira integrations

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Savio?
paragraphs: Savio is a feedback management platform that centralizes customer feature requests pulled from tools like Slack, HubSpot, Intercom, Zendesk, Salesforce, and Help Scout into a single, searchable system.

Instead of relying on spreadsheets or scattered channels, teams use Savio to sort requests by number of requesters, total ARR, and account MRR, then sync status updates back to Jira, Linear, or Shortcut.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Savio is for
paragraphs: Savio suits product, sales, and customer success teams at B2B software companies that want feature requests weighed against revenue data from HubSpot or Salesforce rather than raw vote counts alone.

enrichment.short_description: Canny is an AI feedback platform that captures requests from sales and support calls and ranks them by revenue impact.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Canny captures feedback with AI Autopilot, ranks requests by revenue impact, and offers roadmaps and changelogs. Free plan available.

features.title: AI Autopilot
features.description: Automatically captures feedback from sales calls, support tickets, and other conversations without manual entry.

features.title: Centralized feedback repository
features.description: Aggregates customer requests from Slack, Gong, Intercom, Salesforce, HubSpot, and more into one board.

features.title: Revenue-based prioritization
features.description: Ranks feature requests by revenue impact and customer demand.

features.title: Public roadmap
features.description: Shares planned, in-progress, and shipped features with customers.

features.title: Product changelog
features.description: Announces released features back to the users who requested them.

features.title: Feedback portal
features.description: Collects structured suggestions directly from customers through a dedicated channel.

pros: Free plan available with no credit card required
AI Autopilot reduces manual feedback logging from calls and tickets
Broad integration list including Salesforce, HubSpot, Gong, Slack, Jira, and Zendesk
Used at scale, with Canny citing 100K+ registered companies
cons: Business-tier pricing (CRM integrations, SSO) is custom and not published
Free plan is capped at 25 tracked users
Pro plan billed monthly (~$95/month) costs more than the discounted annual rate ($79/month)

faqs.question: Does Canny have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Canny offers a free plan supporting up to 25 tracked users with unlimited feedback and access to Autopilot AI.

faqs.question: How much does Canny's Pro plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan is $79/month when billed yearly (about $95/month billed monthly) and supports 100+ tracked users.

faqs.question: What does Canny's AI Autopilot do?
faqs.answer: It automatically captures feedback from sales calls, support conversations, and other customer communications instead of requiring manual entry.

faqs.question: What integrations does Canny support?
faqs.answer: Canny integrates with Slack, Gong, Intercom, Salesforce, HubSpot, Zapier, GitHub, Zendesk, Jira, Asana, and ClickUp, among 20+ platforms.

use_cases.title: Revenue-informed roadmapping
use_cases.description: Product teams rank features by revenue impact instead of raw vote counts alone.

use_cases.title: Sales feature-gap tracking
use_cases.description: Sales organizations quantify feature gaps encountered with prospects.

use_cases.title: Customer success expansion signals
use_cases.description: CS managers spot expansion and churn-prevention opportunities from aggregated feedback.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Canny?
paragraphs: Canny is a customer feedback platform that captures, organizes, and prioritizes feature requests to help product teams decide what to build next.

Its AI Autopilot feature automatically pulls feedback out of sales calls, support conversations, and other customer communications, while a revenue-weighted prioritization model helps teams rank requests by business impact rather than vote count alone.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Canny is for
paragraphs: Canny fits product teams, sales organizations, and customer success managers who want a shared, revenue-aware view of customer feedback, plus a public roadmap and changelog to close the loop with users.

enrichment.short_description: Frill is a customer feedback platform with an ideas board, public roadmap, changelog, and surveys, embeddable on any site.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Frill lets teams collect ideas, run a public roadmap, publish changelogs, and send surveys, with plans starting at $25/month.

features.title: Ideas board
features.description: Customers vote, comment, and follow feature requests.

features.title: Public roadmap
features.description: Drag-and-drop, reprioritizable roadmap that can be embedded on a website.

features.title: Announcements and changelog
features.description: Release notifications shown through an embeddable widget.

features.title: Surveys
features.description: NPS, CSAT, polls, and open feedback surveys.

features.title: Priority matrix
features.description: Visual 2D benefit-vs-cost analysis to help weigh ideas.

features.title: White labeling
features.description: Removes Frill branding and adds a custom domain on higher tiers.

features.title: SSO and custom domains
features.description: Enterprise authentication options for larger teams.

pros: 14-day free trial with no credit card required
Wide integration catalog, cited by Frill as 100+ integrations including Jira, Slack, Zendesk, and Linear
All paid tiers include unlimited teammates, tracked users, SSO, webhooks, and API access
Priority matrix gives a visual way to weigh ideas by benefit vs. cost
cons: No permanently free plan; only a 14-day trial is offered
Entry-level Startup plan is capped at 50 ideas and 1 survey
White labeling and privacy controls require the pricier Growth tier ($149/month) or above

faqs.question: Does Frill have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, Frill does not list a permanently free plan; it offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What's included in Frill's cheapest plan?
faqs.answer: The Startup plan ($25/month) includes 50 ideas, 1 survey, and unlimited admins and widgets.

faqs.question: Can I remove Frill branding?
faqs.answer: Yes, white labeling to remove Frill branding is available as an add-on and is included starting at the Growth tier.

faqs.question: Does Frill integrate with other tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, Frill states it offers 100+ integrations, including Jira, Slack, Zendesk, and Linear.

use_cases.title: Public roadmap communication
use_cases.description: Teams share what's planned, in progress, and shipped with customers via an embeddable roadmap.

use_cases.title: Structured customer surveys
use_cases.description: Teams run NPS, CSAT, and open feedback surveys alongside idea collection.

use_cases.title: Idea collection widget
use_cases.description: Sites embed a voting board directly into their product or marketing site.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Frill?
paragraphs: Frill is an all-in-one customer feedback platform that lets product teams collect ideas, run a public roadmap, publish changelogs, and send surveys, all embeddable directly inside a product or website.
Paid plans scale from a $25/month Startup tier up to a $349/month Enterprise tier with SOC2 compliance, audit logs, and IP whitelisting, and every paid plan includes SSO, webhooks, and API access.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Frill is for
paragraphs: Frill suits product teams and SaaS companies that want a single embeddable widget for feedback collection, roadmap sharing, and release announcements without stitching together separate tools.

enrichment.short_description: Nolt is a feedback board and roadmap tool for collecting, prioritizing, and acting on user feedback, from $29/month per board.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Nolt offers feedback boards, roadmaps, and changelogs with SSO and integrations like Slack and Jira, starting at $29/month per board.

features.title: Feedback boards
features.description: Customizable public boards with voting and commenting to collect and rank ideas.

features.title: Roadmap
features.description: Shareable roadmap used to prioritize feedback and communicate plans.

features.title: Privacy controls
features.description: Boards can be public, private to specific users, or password-protected, with domain-restricted access.

features.title: SSO
features.description: Supports SAML 2.0 and other single sign-on providers.

features.title: Moderation tools
features.description: Manual moderation, a profanity filter, user banning, and conversation locking.

features.title: Data export
features.description: Feedback board data can be exported to Google Sheets or Microsoft Excel.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with Slack, Discord, GitHub, and Trello on the Essential plan, plus Zapier, Intercom, Microsoft Teams, Linear, Monday, Asana, and Jira on Pro.

pros: Unlimited users, content, and admins on every plan
Strong privacy and access controls, including password-protected boards, domain restriction, and SSO
10-day free trial of the Pro plan with no credit card required
Built-in moderation tools such as a profanity filter, banning, and manual approval
cons: No free plan; the cheapest plan is $29/month for a single board with no third-party integrations
Zapier and most integrations (Jira, Linear, Asana, Monday) are limited to the $69/month Pro plan
More than 5 boards or custom branding requires custom Enterprise pricing

faqs.question: Does Nolt have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, Nolt has no free plan; new boards start with a 10-day free trial of the Pro plan and no credit card is required.

faqs.question: How much does Nolt cost?
faqs.answer: The Essential plan is $29/month for one board, the Pro plan is $69/month for up to 5 boards, and Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: Can Nolt boards be private?
faqs.answer: Yes, boards can be made public, private to specific users, or password-protected, with access restricted by email domain.

faqs.question: What integrations does the Essential plan include?
faqs.answer: The Essential plan includes Slack, Discord, GitHub, and Trello; integrations like Jira, Linear, and Zapier require the Pro plan.

use_cases.title: Public feedback board
use_cases.description: Companies let users submit and vote on feature ideas through a branded board.

use_cases.title: Roadmap communication
use_cases.description: Teams share a public roadmap of planned and in-progress work.

use_cases.title: Restricted-access feedback
use_cases.description: Teams collect feedback from a private, domain-restricted, or password-protected audience.

features: Intercom, Microsoft Teams, Linear, Monday, Asana, Jira

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Nolt?
paragraphs: Nolt is a feedback board and roadmap platform that helps teams collect user feedback, plan work, and keep customers informed through a public roadmap and changelog.
Plans start at $29/month for a single board on the Essential tier, rising to $69/month for up to 5 boards with a wider integration set on the Pro tier; there is no permanently free plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Nolt is for
paragraphs: Nolt fits product and support teams that want granular privacy controls, such as password-protected or domain-restricted boards, alongside standard voting and roadmap features.

enrichment.short_description: Sleekplan is an AI-powered feedback platform with boards, roadmaps, changelogs, and NPS surveys, with a free Indie plan.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Sleekplan collects feedback, auto-categorizes it with AI, and builds roadmaps and changelogs, with a free plan and paid tiers from $13/month.

features.title: Feedback boards
features.description: Public or private boards with voting, comments, and AI auto-categorization.

features.title: Roadmap
features.description: Drag-and-drop prioritization, shared publicly or kept internal.

features.title: Changelog
features.description: Release notes with reactions, embeds, and an in-app widget.

features.title: NPS and surveys
features.description: Targeted pulse surveys with sentiment scoring.

features.title: AI automation
features.description: Duplicate merging, tagging, and triage of feedback across channels.

features.title: PM Agent
features.description: Scheduled, multi-step automation for recurring product-management tasks.

features.title: MCP server
features.description: Integrates with Claude and Cursor.

pros: Free-forever Indie plan with unlimited feedback and subscribers
30-day free trial with no credit card required on paid plans
AI-assisted duplicate merging, tagging, and triage reduce manual moderation work
Reports 99.99% uptime and GDPR compliance
cons: Free Indie plan is limited to a single-admin workspace
AI credits are capped by plan (1,000/month on Starter, 5,000/month on Business)
Branding removal and custom domain require the Business tier or above

faqs.question: Is Sleekplan free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Indie plan is free forever with unlimited feedback and subscribers, though limited to a single-admin workspace.

faqs.question: Does Sleekplan offer a free trial on paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, every paid plan includes a 30-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How does Sleekplan use AI?
faqs.answer: Sleekplan uses AI to auto-categorize feedback, merge duplicates, tag and triage items, run its PM Agent automation, and connect via an MCP server to Claude and Cursor.

faqs.question: What does the Starter plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan is $13/month billed yearly and includes 3 team seats, CSAT/NPS with 1,000 AI credits, SSO, API access, and webhooks.

use_cases.title: Startup feedback collection
use_cases.description: Small teams start on the free Indie plan and upgrade as they grow.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted feedback triage
use_cases.description: Teams use AI to merge duplicate requests and tag incoming feedback automatically.

use_cases.title: Customer sentiment tracking
use_cases.description: Teams run NPS and CSAT surveys with sentiment scoring and topic analytics.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Sleekplan?
paragraphs: Sleekplan is a feedback management platform that collects customer feedback from multiple channels, uses AI to find themes and merge duplicates, and helps teams publish roadmaps and changelogs.
It offers a free-forever Indie plan for single-admin teams, plus paid tiers starting at $13/month that add AI credits, surveys, SSO, and API access.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Sleekplan is for
paragraphs: Sleekplan suits product teams that want AI-assisted feedback triage and are comparing free-tier feedback tools before committing to a paid plan.

enrichment.short_description: Upvoty is a feedback board and roadmap platform with AI duplicate detection and plans starting at $15/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Upvoty offers feedback boards, public roadmaps, and AI duplicate detection (Merge AI), with plans from $15/month and a 14-day free trial.

features.title: Feedback boards
features.description: Voting, tags, filters, and moderation tools for collecting ideas.

features.title: Public roadmap
features.description: Displays planned, in-progress, and completed items.

features.title: Automated changelog
features.description: Notifies voters automatically when a requested feature ships.

features.title: Merge AI
features.description: AI-powered detection of duplicate feedback posts.

features.title: SSO and private boards
features.description: User SSO plus password-protected board options.

features.title: Custom domain and branding
features.description: Hosts feedback boards on a custom domain with branded styling.

features.title: Multi-language support
features.description: Supports 20+ languages for feedback boards.

pros: 14-day free trial with no credit card required on all plans
AI-powered duplicate detection (Merge AI) reduces manual triage
Custom domain, branding removal, and SSO available starting on the entry Power plan
Supports 20+ languages for multi-region feedback boards
cons: No free plan; the cheapest plan is $15/month
Multiple projects require the top Hyper plan ($49/month); lower tiers are limited to one project
Enterprise pricing (SAML SSO, dedicated success manager) is not published

faqs.question: Does Upvoty have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, Upvoty has no free plan, but all plans include a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What does Upvoty's Power plan include?
faqs.answer: The Power plan ($15/month) includes 1 project, unlimited boards and tracked users, a public roadmap, custom branding, a custom domain, custom SSO, and API access.

faqs.question: What is Merge AI?
faqs.answer: Merge AI is Upvoty's feature for automatically detecting duplicate feedback posts.

faqs.question: Can I run multiple projects on Upvoty?
faqs.answer: Unlimited projects require the Hyper plan ($49/month); the Power and Super plans are limited to one project.

use_cases.title: Multi-language feedback collection
use_cases.description: Global teams run feedback boards in 20+ languages.

use_cases.title: Automated release communication
use_cases.description: Teams notify voters automatically when a requested feature ships.

use_cases.title: Branded feedback portal
use_cases.description: Teams host a fully branded feedback board on their own custom domain.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Upvoty?
paragraphs: Upvoty is a feedback management platform that lets product teams collect user input through voting boards, prioritize requests, and share a public roadmap and changelog.
Its Merge AI feature automatically flags duplicate feedback posts, and plans start at $15/month with a 14-day free trial requiring no credit card.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Upvoty is for
paragraphs: Upvoty suits product teams, including international and multi-language audiences, that want a branded, custom-domain feedback board with automated duplicate detection.

enrichment.short_description: Featurebase combines an omnichannel support inbox, AI support agent, and feedback/roadmap tools, with a free plan.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Featurebase unifies a support inbox, AI agent Fibi, and feedback/roadmap tools; a free plan is available, paid plans start at $29/seat/month.

features.title: Omnichannel inbox
features.description: Unifies in-app, Slack, Discord, and email conversations in one interface.

features.title: Fibi AI agent
features.description: Automated issue resolution to reduce support workload, available on paid tiers.

features.title: Feedback hub
features.description: Collects user feedback with voting and a public roadmap.

features.title: AI feedback prioritization
features.description: Surfaces high-impact product opportunities from collected feedback.

features.title: Help center
features.description: AI-native documentation with multi-language support and search.

features.title: Changelog
features.description: Public changelog pages and an in-app widget with release segmentation.

features.title: Workflows and automations
features.description: SLA management, trial extensions, and discount offers on the Professional tier and above.

pros: Free plan available for individuals, including live chat and a unified inbox
Reports SOC 2, GDPR, and CCPA compliance
Startup program offers an 86% discount plus a year of free Fibi AI for eligible early-stage companies
Broad integration set including Slack, Discord, Jira, HubSpot, Zendesk, Linear, GitHub, ClickUp, and Zapier
cons: Free plan excludes AI support and is limited to 1 seat
AI resolutions are billed per use ($0.49 each) in addition to per-seat pricing
Slack support and workflow automation require the pricier Professional plan or above

faqs.question: Is Featurebase free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Featurebase offers a free plan for 1 seat with live chat, a unified inbox, and mobile app access, though without AI support.

faqs.question: How does Featurebase price AI support?
faqs.answer: Paid plans charge $0.49 per AI resolution in addition to the per-seat monthly price.

faqs.question: What's included in the Growth plan?
faqs.answer: The Growth plan ($29/seat/month billed yearly) adds the Fibi AI agent, email support, AI Copilot, AI replies and macros, and custom domains.

faqs.question: Does Featurebase offer startup discounts?
faqs.answer: Yes, startups under 2 years old with fewer than 6 employees can get an 86% discount on Professional features plus a year of free Fibi AI.

use_cases.title: Unified customer support inbox
use_cases.description: Support teams manage in-app, Slack, Discord, and email tickets in one place.

use_cases.title: Public feedback and roadmap
use_cases.description: Product teams collect votes and publish a public roadmap.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted ticket resolution
use_cases.description: Teams offload repetitive support tickets to the Fibi AI agent.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Featurebase?
paragraphs: Featurebase combines a customer support inbox, an AI support agent called Fibi, and feedback/roadmap tools into a single platform, covering in-app, Slack, Discord, and email channels.
It offers a free plan for individuals, with paid plans starting at $29/seat/month (billed yearly) plus a per-resolution charge for AI-handled support tickets.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Featurebase is for
paragraphs: Featurebase suits support and product teams that want to combine ticketing, AI-assisted resolution, and public feedback/roadmap management in one tool, from solo founders on the free plan to multi-team organizations on Professional or Enterprise.

enrichment.short_description: UserVoice is a customer intelligence platform that aggregates feedback and uses AI to weight requests by revenue impact.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: UserVoice aggregates customer feedback across Salesforce, Zendesk, and Gong, using AI to reveal revenue-weighted product insights.

features.title: Multi-source aggregation
features.description: Connects Salesforce, Zendesk, Gainsight, Gong, Slack, Jira, and Azure DevOps into one feedback stream.

features.title: Revenue weighting
features.description: Correlates customer problems with deal size, expansion, and retention.

features.title: AI theme detection
features.description: Automatically surfaces emerging trends and customer needs from feedback data.

features.title: Role-specific dashboards
features.description: Tailored views for product, sales, customer success, and support teams.

features.title: Churn signal detection
features.description: Flags early indicators that UserVoice states can appear 6-12 months before a customer churns.

pros: Deep integrations with enterprise tools like Salesforce, Gong, Gainsight, and Azure DevOps
AI-driven theme detection and revenue weighting go beyond simple upvote counting
SOC 2 Type 2 certified, and states GDPR and WCAG compliance
30-day hands-on trial available via a guided demo workspace
cons: No public pricing; plans require booking a demo
Pricing is usage-based (feedback volume, tools, integrations) rather than a flat published rate
No self-serve signup; onboarding goes through a sales/demo process

faqs.question: Does UserVoice publish its pricing?
faqs.answer: No, UserVoice does not list plan prices publicly; pricing is based on feedback volume, tools used, and integrations connected.

faqs.question: Can I try UserVoice before buying?
faqs.answer: Yes, booking a demo sets up a workspace so a team can try UserVoice hands-on for 30 days.

faqs.question: What CRMs and support tools does UserVoice integrate with?
faqs.answer: UserVoice integrates with Salesforce, Zendesk, Gainsight, Gong, Slack, Jira, and Azure DevOps.

faqs.question: What is UserVoice's churn signal detection?
faqs.answer: It is an AI feature designed to surface signals that UserVoice states can appear 6-12 months before a customer churns.

use_cases.title: Revenue-weighted prioritization
use_cases.description: Product teams rank customer problems by correlation with deal size and retention.

use_cases.title: Churn risk detection
use_cases.description: Customer success teams monitor early churn signals surfaced from feedback data.

use_cases.title: Cross-functional feedback dashboards
use_cases.description: Sales, support, and product teams each get a tailored view of the same underlying feedback data.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is UserVoice?
paragraphs: UserVoice is a customer intelligence platform that aggregates feedback signals from tools like Salesforce, Zendesk, Gong, Slack, Jira, and Azure DevOps, then applies AI analysis to surface revenue-weighted insights.

Rather than ranking requests by vote count alone, UserVoice correlates customer problems with deal size, expansion, and retention, and includes churn signal detection intended to flag risk months in advance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who UserVoice is for
paragraphs: UserVoice targets product, customer success, revenue operations, and sales teams at B2B software companies that need feedback prioritized by business impact and are comfortable going through a sales/demo process rather than self-serve signup.

name: CommandBar
skip_reason: CommandBar rebranded to Command AI and was acquired by Amplitude in October 2024 (TechCrunch, Amplitude press release); the commandbar.com domain now 301-redirects to amplitude.com/guides-and-surveys, so there is no standalone CommandBar product/site left to enrich.

name: Dopt
skip_reason: Dopt was acquihired by Airtable in July 2024 and shut down its service on August 15, 2024 (TechCrunch, Airtable newsroom); dopt.com now 301-redirects to airtable.com, confirming the product is discontinued.
